title: Tasmanian tigers discovered in Indigenous rock art in Australia, suggesting these marsupials lived there much longer than thought
summary: Around 14 new rock-art depictions of thylacines, also known as Tasmanian tigers, have been found in northern Australia.

title: What would happen to Earth if the sun suddenly vanished?
summary: Earth is habitable due to warmth from the sun. So what would happen if the sun disappeared?

title: How to catch the full 'Pink Moon' in April followed by a 'Blue Moon' in May
summary: April's full moon, known as the Pink Moon, determines the dates of Passover and Easter. This year, it rises on April 1.

title: Here's the stargazing gear you need for April 2026 — don't miss the Lyrids, two comets and the full moon
summary: These are our top camera, binocular and telescope recommendations for April's stargazing events, including the Lyrid meteor shower and two bright comets.

title: DNA shed by every living thing is lurking in the environment — and it could tell us how Earth is changing in real time
summary: Environments are littered with the DNA of the creatures that inhabit them. Analyzing it could provide a real-time view of how our planet is changing.

title: Lençóis Maranhenses: Brazil's dune-filled expanse that sits at the intersection of 3 biomes
summary: Lençóis Maranhenses National Park hosts sand-dune fields that fill up with lagoons every wet season, but the reserve also has mangrove swamps where species such as the scarlet ibis thrive.

title: 'Major disruption in Neanderthal history': 65,000 years ago, all Neanderthals in Europe died out except for one lineage
summary: The last Neanderthals to survive in Europe came from a single lineage that survived the worst period of the ice age, ancient DNA reveals.

title: Astronauts will 'absolutely be test subjects': NASA's moon plans pose big questions — and big risks
summary: Experts say building a lunar colony within the next decade, as NASA and Elon Musk want to, will require finding solutions to problems we don't yet fully understand.

title: 18 million-year-old fossils of ape found in Africa, but in an unexpected place
summary: The ancestor of apes was long thought to come from East Africa, but newly discovered fossils in Egypt may prompt a rethink.

title: Astronauts may struggle to reproduce in outer space, study suggests — what does that mean for the future of space colonization?
summary: A new study found that microgravity simulated on Earth hindered sperm cell movement, egg fertilization and embryo development ‪—‬ findings that have serious implications for the future of space colonization.

title: 1,000-year-old altar and human sacrifices from Toltec Empire discovered in Mexico
summary: Archaeologists found the altar and human bones during a construction project near Tula, an ancient city that was the capital of the pre-Hispanic Toltec Empire.

title: Chinese lander reveals giant 'cavity' of radiation between Earth and the moon — and it could change how lunar exploration is done
summary: A new study using data from China's Chang'e-4 moon lander found an area of reduced radiation from cosmic rays near the moon. The findings could be used to improve the safety of lunar explorations.

title: NASA announces 'near‑impossible' space plans, including $20B moon base and humanity's first nuclear-powered interplanetary spacecraft
summary: NASA's Gateway lunar space station won't launch next year in new changes to the Artemis program, which include ramping up development of a $20 billion moon base and a nuclear-powered 'Freedom' spacecraft.

title: Massive Iron Age hoards discovered in England may be from funeral of powerful Celtic queen

title: An anomaly in Mars' mantle could trigger volcanoes to erupt — and may be causing the whole planet to spin faster
summary: Data from NASA's InSight mission suggests the Red Planet's Tharsis region is more active than previously thought and may be why Mars is spinning more quickly over time.

title: Colorado River negotiations have stalled among 7 states and water is scarce. What happens next?
